summary: Tested signup form validation fixes. Found and fixed a critical bug: validateSignupForm() was filtering schema fields with f.required, which excluded the address block (address has required:false at top-level even though sub-fields like country/city/postal are required). Fixed by iterating all enabled fields and handling address blocks with their own required flags. After fix, all validation scenarios pass correctly.
passed_tests: Signup: Country empty → 'Please fill in: Country' toast appears (PASS after fix)
Signup: Full Name whitespace-only → 'Please fill in: Full Name' toast appears (PASS)
Signup: City whitespace-only → 'Please fill in: City' toast appears (PASS)
Signup: Postal whitespace-only → 'Please fill in: Postal Code' toast appears (PASS)
Signup: Form correctly stays on /signup page when validation fails (PASS)
Code review: ProductDetail.tsx intake form whitespace trim is in place at lines 218-222 (trimmedVal = val.trim())
Code review: Cart.tsx sectionRequiredFieldsMissing whitespace trim is in place at lines 83-95 (val.trim() === '')
failed_tests: Intake form end-to-end test: No products have intake_schema configured in this environment - cannot test. Verified via code review only.
action_items: CRITICAL BUG FIXED by tester: validateSignupForm() in Signup.tsx was iterating schema fields with filter 'f.required', but the address field in the schema has required:false at top level even though sub-fields (country, city, postal) have required:true in address_config. Fixed filter to iterate all enabled fields and check address sub-fields separately.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED: Signup.tsx validateSignupForm() previously used schema.filter(f => f.enabled !== false && f.required) - this excluded the address block because it has required:false. Fixed to iterate all enabled fields and handle address type separately from regular field required check.

Verified fix: The address block in automate-accounts tenant schema has {required: false, type: 'address', address_config: {country: {required: true}, city: {required: true}, postal: {required: true}, line1: {required: true}}}

NOTE: The test partner code 'AUTOMATE' provided in requirements does NOT exist - only 'automate-accounts' is valid. The 'automate-accounts' code is reserved for platform admin; customer registration via signup returns 403. For real end-to-end customer signup tests, a dedicated customer tenant code is needed.
ProductDetail.tsx whitespace trim: Uses trimmedVal = typeof val === 'string' ? val.trim() : val - correct
Cart.tsx sectionRequiredFieldsMissing: Returns true when val.trim() === '' - correct

success_rate.frontend: 100% (4/4 signup validation scenarios pass after fix, intake form not testable in this environment)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Key finding: The signup form validation fix in validateSignupForm() had a bug where the address schema block was excluded because it has required:false at the top level. The fix changes the filter from 'f.required' to handle address type separately. This fix was applied. All signup form validation tests pass after the fix: Country empty, whitespace-only full_name/city/postal all correctly show 'Please fill in: ...' toast. Note: 'AUTOMATE' partner code doesn't exist in this environment - use 'automate-accounts' to access signup form. For end-to-end signup test with actual registration, use 'apex-holding' tenant code (real customer tenant). The automate-accounts code is platform admin reserved and returns 403 on registration attempt.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: validateSignupForm() in Signup.tsx filtered schema fields with `schema.filter(f => f.enabled !== false && f.required)`. The address block in the tenant's signup_form_schema has `required: false` at the top level (while individual sub-fields like country, city, postal have `required: true` in address_config). Because the address field was filtered out, none of the address sub-field validations ever ran - meaning Country, City, Postal could all be empty and the form would submit.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed filter to iterate all enabled fields: `schema.filter(f => f.enabled !== false)`. Address blocks are always checked via address_config.*.required. Non-address fields are only checked if field.required is true.
reproduction_steps: 1. Navigate to /signup with aa_partner_code=automate-accounts in localStorage
2. Fill all text fields (full_name, email, password, company_name, job_title, phone, line1, city, postal)
BEFORE FIX: Form submits to API, gets 403 (no JS validation error)
AFTER FIX: Toast shows 'Please fill in: Country' and form stays on /signup
